evidence: "Samurai Champloo" modernizes the samurai story, throwing in hip-hop elements...
"Full Metal Panic" perfectly exemplifies the series' best qualities: the ability to seemingly combine genres...
The story doesn't hold as tightly together, and goes all over the place...
The series doesn't shy away from more abstract or philosophical ideas about what is right or wrong...
The first half of the 26-episode series drags along with episode after unconnected episode...
This may be the slowest disc of the series, which is saying a lot considering the dripping-molasses pace...
The animation in this series is superb.
The art is at its best since the pilot episode, and the music is also in top form.
anything directed by Shinichiro Watanabe will always have the label "from the makers of Cowboy Bebop."
The "Champloo" style is cool. Disc-scratching, beats, and blurry-style camera movement make for awesome imagery...
The animation in this series is superb. Experienced viewers will be in awe of the way the artists have used CGI...
The three-part "Homelands" story is arguably the best the series has to offer, an experience that will stick strongly...
The tragedy can bring tears at its most poignant moments.

summary: The user has a strong preference for anime, particularly those that blend genres and offer high-quality animation. They appreciate emotional depth and sensory experiences in storytelling, while showing mixed feelings towards narrative complexity and pacing.
